headline: Micron, SanDisk, and Western Digital Fall 7% as Memory Rally Cools
summary: Shares of Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) are down 7% to $1,130 in early Friday trading. SanDisk (NASDAQ:SNDK) is slipping 7% to $2,176, while Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) is declining 6% to $626. The coordinated retreat across the three biggest U.S. memory and storage names suggests profit-taking rather than a thesis-breaking shift. The trio had ripped higher into ... headline: The bottleneck is the trade: Here's what you need to know
summary: Memory just decoupled from the AI selloff — hot inflation data sank the broader market and Nvidia fell below $200, but Micron hit an all-time high. The trade has flipped: Wall Street’s skipping the hyperscaler paying for AI and buying the company controlling the chokepoint instead. CNBC's Kristina Partsinevelos reports.
